spec.reasoning: The instruction references specific cell addresses E2:E15 and column letters B, C, E. Since inserting a blank row would shift rows and break the E2:E15 reference (as seen in Attempt 1), and inserting a distractor column before E would shift the target column (as seen in Attempt 2), I use only safe perturbations: (1) Lexical renames of all column headers and the sheet name to semantically equivalent alternatives — 'Time' → 'Timestamp', 'Actual Value' → 'Observed Value', 'Forecast Value' → 'Projected Value', ' Forecast VALUE' → ' Projected TOTAL', and 'Sheet1' → 'DataSheet'. These break hardcoded name lookups while preserving meaning. (2) A distractor column 'Adjusted Value' placed AFTER the last column (after column E), so it goes into column F. This does NOT shift any existing columns, preserving the B, C, E references in the instruction. The distractor name is plausibly similar to existing columns but clearly different.
